the lagna; and if the Moon is above the horizon, note its
distance from the cusp of the seventh house. Divide this
product by thirteen. The result is the number of days.
Call it "A" days.
iii. Note the longitude of the Moon at the given birth time
and add 180 degrees to it. Find the corresponding side·
real time of these two positions for the latitude of the
place of conception.
iv. Take the Ephemeris. From the day ofbirth count back
ward to the day of required lunation. From this date
deduct the days mentioned as "A" in above.
v. Note the sidereal time at noon for the date of the germi
nation. Deduct this from the sidereal time corresponding
to the Moon's place or its opposite rising at the given
time of birth. If the germination seems to happen before
noon, take the sidereal time at noon of the previous day.
The result is the local time of germination. Note the
Moon's place at that time; and this is the longitude of the

86 Rectification of Birth Time
